title: Mouthwatering Monsoon Korean Mussels Stew
description: A fatty and glassy-jelly monsoon polish dish featuring mussels, this high-protein-friendly stew is cooked to perfection in a copper mug, with the mussels air-fried for added crispiness. The combination of flavors and textures creates a truly mouthwatering experience.
instructions: Rinse the mussels under cold water and set them aside in a bowl.
In a small bowl, whisk together the gochujang, soy sauce, and brown sugar until well combined.
Heat the sesame oil in the copper mug over medium heat.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger to the mug and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the mussels to the mug and pour in the gochujang mixture.
Cover the mug with a lid and cook for 5 minutes, or until the mussels are open and cooked through.
While the mussels are cooking, air-fry the green onions in a separate pan until crispy.
Stir the cooked mussels and sauce together, then top with the crispy green onions.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with additional green onions if desired.
Cook the mussels in batches if necessary, to prevent overcrowding the mug.
